zh: 不关你的事。是我自己不小心。而且，能帮到你，我很高兴。
en: It's not your fault. I was careless. And... I'm glad I could help you.

zh: 林辰。
en: Lin Chen.

zh: 以前我总是觉得，什么事都要靠自己，别人都靠不住。
en: I used to think I had to do everything myself, that no one else could be counted on.

zh: 但是遇到你之后，我才发现，原来有人可以依靠的感觉，这么好。
en: But after meeting you... I realized how good it feels to have someone to lean on.

zh: 原来……我不是一个人在战斗。
en: Turns out... I'm not fighting alone.

zh: 你从来都不是一个人。以后，我会一直陪着你。
en: You've never been alone. From now on, I'll always be here with you.

zh: 时间不早了，我该回去了。你好好休息。
en: It's getting late. I should head back. Get some rest.

zh: 对了，下周三我们要去郊区的工厂选布料。等你病好了，我们一起去。
en: Oh, and next Wednesday we're going to the factory in the suburbs to pick fabrics. Once you're better, we'll go together.

zh: 好。
en: Okay.

zh: 早啊！
en: Morning!

zh: 早。东西都带齐了吗？
en: Morning. Got everything?

zh: 带齐了！色卡、样布、笔记本，一样都没少。
en: All set! Color swatches, fabric samples, notebook — nothing missing.

zh: 今天我们要去郊区的那家布料厂，他们家的蕾丝质量是整个山城最好的。
en: Today we're heading to that fabric mill in the suburbs. Their lace is the best in all of Chongqing.

zh: 好，那我们走吧。
en: Alright, let's go.

zh: 安小姐来了啊！这次要找什么布料？
en: Miss An! Here again! What kind of fabric are you after this time?

zh: 王老板好。我要找一款黑色的哑光蕾丝，还有银色的刺绣线。
en: Hello, Boss Wang. I'm looking for a matte black lace, and some silver embroidery thread.

zh: 就是上次我跟你说的那款，要最柔软的那种，不能扎皮肤。
en: The one I mentioned last time — the softest kind you've got. Nothing that'll irritate the skin.

zh: （拿起一卷蕾丝仔细地摸着，眉头微微皱起）这个不行，太硬了。穿在身上会不舒服。
en: （Picks up a roll of lace, feeling it carefully, brow furrowing slightly）No good. Too stiff. It'd be uncomfortable to wear.

zh: 还有别的吗？
en: Got anything else?

zh: 有有有，这边还有一批进口的，你看看。
en: Yes, yes — we've got an imported batch over here. Take a look.

zh: （拿起另一卷蕾丝，眼睛一下子亮了）就是这个！这个手感太好了！
en: （Picks up another roll of lace, eyes lighting up instantly）This is it! The texture is perfect!

zh: 王老板，就要这个，给我来五百米。还有银色的刺绣线，也要最好的那种。
en: Boss Wang, I'll take this one — five hundred meters. And the silver embroidery thread, the best you've got.

zh: 没问题！安小姐的眼光就是好，这批货刚到，你是第一个看上的。
en: No problem! Miss An has an eye for quality — this batch just arrived, and you're the first to spot it.

zh: 年纪轻轻就这么能干，自己做品牌，真了不起啊。
en: So young and already running your own label. That's really impressive.

zh: （耳朵一下子红了）哪里哪里，都是王老板照顾。
en: （Ears turning red）Oh, not at all — I couldn't have done it without your support, Boss Wang.

zh: 好了，王老板，合同签好了。下周三之前能发货吗？
en: Alright, Boss Wang, the contract's signed. Can you ship it before next Wednesday?

zh: 没问题！一定准时送到。
en: No problem! It'll arrive right on time.

zh: （伸了个懒腰）呼……终于搞定了！
en: （Stretching）Phew... finally done!

zh: 没想到这么顺利，比我预想的早了好几个小时。
en: Didn't expect it to go this smoothly — we finished hours earlier than I thought.

zh: 那个……今天下午没事了。
en: So, uh... I've got nothing on this afternoon.

zh: 正好我也饿了。我知道老城区有一家特别地道的九宫格老火锅，要不要去试试？
en: I'm getting hungry too. There's this really authentic nine-grid old-school hotpot place in the old town. Want to check it out?

zh: 好啊好啊！我早就想吃火锅了！
en: Yes, yes! I've been craving hotpot forever!

zh: （连忙清了清嗓子）咳……既然你这么诚心邀请，那我就勉强陪你去吧。
en: （Quickly clears throat）Ahem... well, since you're asking so sincerely, I suppose I can reluctantly keep you company.

zh: 那我先回学校了，还有点作业要写。
en: I'll head back to campus first, got some homework to finish.

zh: ……哦，好吧。那……宣传册的事，我们微信联系。
en: ...Oh. Okay. Then... I'll message you on WeChat about the brochure.

zh: 你来啦。快坐吧，我已经点好菜了。
en: You're here. Sit down — I already ordered.

zh: 不好意思，让你久等了。
en: Sorry to keep you waiting.

zh: 哼，知道就好。今天主要是讨论宣传册的事，你别想太多。
en: Hmph, good that you know. Today's mainly about the brochure, so don't get any ideas.

zh: 好，听你的。
en: Alright, whatever you say.

zh: 哇……好多人啊。
en: Wow... it's packed.

zh: 这家店开了二十多年了，味道特别正宗，所以每天都爆满。
en: This place has been around for over twenty years — the flavors are legit, so it's packed every single day.

zh: （看着桌上的九宫格锅底）这个……为什么要分成九个格子啊？
en: （Looking at the nine-grid pot on the table）This... why are there nine compartments?

zh: 不同的格子温度不一样，中间的火最大，适合涮毛肚鸭肠这种需要快煮的，边上的火小，适合煮土豆藕片这种需要慢炖的。
en: Each compartment has a different temperature. The center's the hottest — good for quick-cook stuff like tripe and duck intestines. The edges are cooler, better for slow-simmer things like potato and lotus root.

zh: 哦……原来是这样。
en: Oh... I see.

zh: 两位要点什么锅底？我们这里有微辣、中辣、特辣。
en: What broth base would you like? We've got mild, medium, and extra-spicy.

zh: （偷偷看了我一眼）那就……特辣吧！我能吃辣！
en: （Steals a glance at me）Then... extra-spicy! I can handle it!

zh: 是吗？我怎么记得上次有人吃小面都被辣得直吐舌头。
en: Oh really? Funny, I seem to remember someone who couldn't even handle spicy noodles without fanning her tongue.

zh: 那是意外！我现在很能吃辣了！
en: That was a fluke! I'm way better with spice now!

zh: 好好好，那就微辣吧。我不能吃太辣。
en: Alright, alright — let's go with mild. I can't handle too much spice.

zh: （偷偷松了一口气）好吧，那就听你的，微辣。
en: （Breathes a quiet sigh of relief）Fine, you win. Mild it is.

zh: 哇……开了开了！
en: Wow... it's boiling!

zh: （拿起筷子就要往锅里放毛肚）这个要煮多久啊？
en: （Picks up chopsticks, ready to drop tripe in）How long does this need to cook?

zh: 等等，我教你。毛肚要七上八下，就是在锅里涮八次，每次提起来一下，这样煮出来的毛肚才会脆。
en: Wait, let me show you. Tripe needs 'seven ups, eight downs' — you dip it in and out eight times. That's what makes it crispy.

zh: （手被我抓住，脸一下子红了）哦……哦好。
en: （I grab her hand, her face instantly turning red）Oh... okay.

zh: （小心翼翼地按照我说的方法涮着毛肚）这样……可以了吗？
en: （Carefully following my method, swishing the tripe）Like... this? Is it ready?

zh: 可以了，尝尝看。
en: Yep, try it.

zh: （咬了一口，眼睛一下子亮了）哇！好好吃！好脆啊！
en: （Takes a bite, eyes lighting up）Wow! This is amazing! So crunchy!

zh: 好吃就多吃点。
en: If you like it, have some more.

zh: 那个……你喜欢吃什么？
en: So... what do you like to eat?

zh: 我都可以，不挑食。
en: Anything, really. I'm not picky.

zh: 那你除了拍照和设计，还喜欢做什么？
en: Apart from photography and design, what else do you like doing?

zh: 喜欢看电影，还有爬山。山城的山很多，周末没事的时候我就会去爬山。
en: I like watching movies, and hiking. There are plenty of mountains in Chongqing — I go hiking on weekends when I'm free.

zh: 你呢？除了设计裙子，还喜欢做什么？
en: What about you? Besides designing dresses, what do you like?

zh: 我喜欢看星星，还有听古典音乐。
en: I like stargazing. And listening to classical music.

zh: 小时候在英国的时候，我家后面有一片很大的草地，晚上我就会躺在草地上看星星。
en: When I was little, back in England, there was a big meadow behind my house. I'd lie on the grass and watch the stars at night.

zh: 那时候的星星特别亮，比山城的亮多了。
en: The stars back then were so bright — way brighter than they are here in Chongqing.

zh: 以后我带你去南山看星星。南山的海拔高，没有光污染，星星也很亮。
en: I'll take you to Nanshan to see the stars someday. It's high up, no light pollution — the stars are really bright there too.

zh: 真的吗？
en: Really?

zh: （喝了一口酸梅汤）其实……我挺喜欢山城的。
en: （Takes a sip of sour plum drink）Actually... I've grown to like Chongqing quite a bit.

zh: 虽然夏天很热，冬天很冷，路也很难走，但这里的人很热情，东西也很好吃。
en: Sure, the summers are sweltering, winters freezing, and the streets are a pain to walk — but people here are warm, and the food's amazing.
